notes: Token/cost quota exhausted: aider hits a hard token-or-cost cap mid-turn (provider HTTP 429 / litellm RateLimitError, or litellm BudgetExceededError) and resolves the turn cleanly to ready ‚Äî it does not silently re-enter working. WHY aider reaches ready: the send loop in coders/base_coder.py doubles retry_delay (start 0.125s) on each retryable error and, once retry_delay > RETRY_TIMEOUT (60s, models.py), sets should_retry=False, writes the cap via io.tool_warning+io.tool_error, and breaks ‚Äî ending the turn (the non-retryable APIError+402 'Insufficient credits' case breaks immediately). io.tool_error/tool_warning route through io._tool_message ‚Üí append_chat_history(blockquote=True), so the surfaced cap lands in .aider.chat.history.md as a `> ‚Ä¶Error‚Ä¶` blockquote line. HOW irrlicht observes it (the #262 path): the aider parser's errorRE (`^>\s*\S*(?:Error|Exception)(?:[: ]|$)`, parser.go:75) matches that blockquote while turnOpen and calls flushErrorTurn (parser.go:196), which emits turn_done with the error as AssistantText and clears turnOpen ‚Äî flipping LastEventType so the state classifier settles working‚Üíready. So the scenario's primary User-observable signal ‚Äî state reaches ready ‚Äî IS observed. SESSION IDENTITY (same as aider/basic-turn and aider/self-correction-iteration): aider stores its transcript as .aider.chat.history.md in the cwd (FilesUnderCWD, RawLineParser), so the daemon keys the WHOLE session by proc-<PID> ‚Äî there is no separate transcript-UUID rollout session and no handoff/reconcile phase. Every event (transcript_new, presession_created, the state arc, pid_discovered, process_exited) lands on the one proc-<PID> row, so turn_start/turn_end/teardown all pin to session_birth via same_session_as. To avoid a transient proc-<PID> presession row stealing identity, session_birth anchors to recording start UNPINNED; turn_start (the first real working) establishes the bindable session and turn_end/teardown chain off session_birth's match. observes:partial (TWO inherent gaps the matrix verdict cites ‚Äî both DOCUMENTED here, neither widens this spec): (1) FEATURE-INVISIBLE: the cap reaches ready via the generic LLM-error blockquote path, so irrlicht CANNOT tell a quota/budget cap apart from any other turn-aborting provider error ‚Äî there is no quota/budget-specific event kind, so this spec asserts only the generic working‚Üíready arc, NOT a distinct quota signal (the scenario-meanings 'budget enforcement' primitive collapses to the standard turn-end path). (2) METRIC-DRIFT: the capped model call never emits a `> Tokens: ‚Ä¶` line, so closeModelCall never runs and flushErrorTurn carries nil Tokens/Contribution (parser.go:194-199) ‚Äî no per-turn token/cost is recorded for the capped turn, only the error text as AssistantText. capabilities.cost_tracking is false; aider has NO native cost-cap feature ‚Äî the budget is enforced provider-side (429) or by litellm (BudgetExceededError). The 'subsequent user prompts stay ready until budget resets' half of the signal is NOT retained as state by the adapter (a fresh `#### ` prompt would re-open a turn and re-attempt), so this spec asserts the capped turn's clean working‚Üíready settle and a final ready, not a sticky budget state. RECORDING DEFERRED ‚Äî BLOCKED ON BOTH auth AND a quota mock (NOT just a driver step type, so NOT a driver_gap): (a) no aider auth in this environment (no provider key; headless aider hangs ~5min on provider OAuth; the rig precheck delegates auth to the user), and (b) there is NO deterministic aider quota mock yet ‚Äî the claudecode mock-anthropic-429 path does NOT transfer: that mock (tools/agent-onboarding/recording/record-token-quota-exhausted.sh) launches `claude --bare` with ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL pointed at the stub, but aider's drivers (drive-aider.sh headless, drive-aider-interactive.sh) wire NO base-URL override and have no `--bare` equivalent, and aider routes every provider through litellm, so even with auth this cell has no deterministic way to elicit a 429/BudgetExceededError on demand. A recording becomes possible only once BOTH (auth) AND (an aider-side quota mock or a driver fixture-injection primitive) exist ‚Äî a developer task. HOW runs WOULD differ once recordable: the exact error wording, sub-second timing, and the up-to-60s RETRY_TIMEOUT backoff window before the cap surfaces are model/provider-dependent; structurally stable is one ready‚Üíworking‚Üíready arc on the one proc-<PID> session with the `> ‚Ä¶Error‚Ä¶` blockquote inside the working span, a single process_exited, and a final ready state ‚Äî never waiting. This expected.jsonl is the DOCUMENTED TARGET the recording will validate against once auth AND a quota mock are available.

text: Session appears in ready within 1s of agent launch
User prompt ‚Üí working; aider is working through the turn when the token/cost cap is hit (conceptual flow steps 1-2)

The cap surfaces as a `> ‚Ä¶Error‚Ä¶` blockquote that aborts the turn; aider emits a turn-end signal and stops ‚Üí working‚Üíready within the daemon's sweep; state never entered waiting (conceptual flow steps 3-4). The quota cap is NOT distinguishable from a generic LLM error here (observes:partial)
Session torn down after the capped turn ‚Üí a single process_exited on the session; state immediately before teardown is ready
